arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line dotnet 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm-symbolizer 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; which pip pip3 2>/dev/null

# Try downloading the libcoreclr.so with debug info from Microsoft symbol server
# BuildId: 4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"

# Try various URL patterns used by the Microsoft symbol server for ELF binaries
echo "=== Trying elf-buildid pattern ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so"
echo ""

echo "=== Trying elf-buildid-sym pattern (debug) ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo ""

echo "=== Trying symstore-style ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so"
echo ""

echo "=== Trying libcoreclr.so.dbg ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so.dbg"
echo ""

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this tombstone. The key frames are from `libcoreclr.so` with BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`.

To symbolicate these, I need to:
1. Find the debug symbols for this specific build of `libcoreclr.so`
2. Use the BuildId to locate the correct symbol file
3. Use a tool like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to resolve the addresses

The BuildId is `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`. 

This is the CoreCLR runtime library for .NET on Android. Microsoft publishes symbol packages for .NET runtime libraries on their symbol server, and I can construct the download URL using the BuildId to fetch the symbols needed for resolving these addresses.

I'm considering a few different approaches to retrieve the debug symbols: using the `dotnet-symbol` tool, querying the Microsoft symbol server directly with the proper ELF BuildId format, or trying debuginfod as a fallback option. I've got the Build ID `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`, so let me try querying the Microsoft symbol server directly with that. I should also check what debugging tools are available on the system—things like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line`—and see if the .NET SDK is installed, since those could help with symbol resolution.
